_instructions: Fill every field marked WRITE with 1-3 sentences of plain English. DO NOT compute numbers — copy them from canonical_input.json exactly. Fields marked COPY contain pre-written text — copy verbatim. Fields marked FIXED are structural — do not modify.
title: Financial Rate Benchmarking Report
subtitle_COPY: Florida Medicaid · Fee-for-Service Claims · 2024 Analysis Period

executive_summary._instructions: 3-5 bullets. Each bullet is ONE sentence. Lead with the finding, not the methodology. Prioritize by revenue impact. Reference specific dollar amounts from canonical_input.
bullets: WRITE: Bullet 1 — the single most important finding (likely H2019 rate outlier)
WRITE: Bullet 2 — the biggest concern (likely T1017 trend or caseload pattern)
WRITE: Bullet 3 — this data covers only professional FFS Medicaid claims ($1.2M) which is a small portion of total operations; ACT and crisis are visible but lack peers
WRITE: Bullet 4 — one strength worth protecting

section_1_about.org_profile_WRITE: WRITE: 2-3 sentences describing what kind of org this is (comprehensive BH system, sparse market, residential taxonomy). Use service_line_breakdown for revenue shares.

four_metrics.rate_per_claim_FIXED: What Medicaid pays your organization, on average, each time you submit a claim for a given service. This is your price signal.
four_metrics.patients_per_clinician_FIXED: How many unique patients each of your billing clinicians serves. This is your caseload signal.
four_metrics.visits_per_patient_FIXED: How many claims are generated per unique patient. This is your treatment engagement signal.
four_metrics.revenue_per_patient_FIXED: Total Medicaid payment received per unique patient for a given service. This is your combined economic signal.

section_1_about.peer_groups_WRITE: WRITE: 2-3 sentences explaining look-alike peers (primary, n=X, similarity range) and the 5 secondary groups. Use canonical_input.look_alike_count and look_alike_similarity_range.
section_1_about.how_to_read_FIXED: In each chart, the diamond marks your organization's value. The blue line marks the peer median. The purple line marks the look-alike peer median. The gray band spans P25 to P75.

badge_legend_FIXED.Strong: Above market median, holding steady or pulling ahead
badge_legend_FIXED.Watch: Above median but converging toward it, or below median but recovering
badge_legend_FIXED.Concern: Below the median on most peer comparisons

section_1_about.key_limitation_FIXED: Rate per claim measures what your organization is paid per claim submission, not per 15-minute unit of service. Organizations that bill longer sessions will show higher per-claim amounts.

section_2_dashboard.coverage_note_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence noting that ACT (26.1%) and crisis (23.1%) have limited or no peer benchmark data.
section_2_dashboard.rows_COPY: COPY dashboard_rows from canonical_input — one row per code with: code, name, service_line, revenue_share, badge_rate, badge_productivity, badge_utilization, badge_revenue, trend_arrow, trend_label

_badge_overrides._instructions: Apply Watch badge where rubric requires: position ≈P50 + trend Converging, or position Below + trend Recovering

section_2_dashboard.h0040_row_COPY: COPY from canonical_input.h0040_summary: code H0040, ACT, rate_per_claim, claims, share_pct. Badges: Limited data.
section_2_dashboard.s9485_row_COPY: COPY from canonical_input.s9485_summary: code S9485, Crisis, rate_per_claim, claims, share_pct. Badges: No peer data.

section_3_deep_dives._instructions: One entry per code. Each has a fixed structure. The WRITE fields are where you add prose.

H2019.rate_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence. Badge from dashboard. Cite payment_per_claim, look_alike_p50_ppc (n=look_alike_n), and one peer p50_ppc with n.
H2019.productivity_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence. Badge. Cite bene_per_clinician vs peer p50_bpc.
H2019.utilization_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence. Badge. Cite claims_per_bene vs peer p50_cpb.
H2019.revenue_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence. Badge. Cite revenue_per_bene vs look_alike_p50_rpb and peer p50_rpb.
H2019.revenue_impact_COPY: COPY: $87,391 annual rate advantage vs look-alike peers (from canonical_input.codes.H2019.revenue_impact_vs_lookalike)
H2019.key_question_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence framing what to investigate. Question not conclusion.
H2019.caveat_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence on the per-claim vs per-unit limitation for this code.

T1015.rate_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence with badge, payment_per_claim, look_alike_p50_ppc, peer p50_ppc.

T1017.productivity_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence. This is the key concern — cite 179.5 vs 275.0.
T1017.revenue_impact_COPY: COPY: $68 (minimal — rate is at look-alike median)
T1017.key_question_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence focusing on the caseload decline, not the rate.

H0031.caveat_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence. Note volume caution (91 claims) and modifier rollup.

H0032.rate_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence. Badge is WATCH (Above but Converging).
H0032.key_question_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence about the converging trend.
H0032.caveat_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence. Note volume caution (68 claims) and modifier rollup.

99214.rate_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence. Badge is WATCH (WELL BELOW but Recovering).
99214.key_question_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence about whether this is a new service or established.
99214.caveat_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence. Note volume caution (45 claims).

section_3_deep_dives.h0040_section_WRITE: WRITE: 3-4 sentences about ACT (H0040). Cite h0040_summary rate_per_claim, claims, share_pct. Note limited peer data (4 look-alikes bill this code).
section_3_deep_dives.s9485_section_WRITE: WRITE: 3-4 sentences about crisis stabilization (S9485). Cite s9485_summary. Include crisis_note from canonical_input explaining why no peers available.

section_4_cross_cutting._instructions: 3 observations that connect patterns across codes.
section_4_cross_cutting.caseload_pattern_WRITE: WRITE: 2-3 sentences connecting the low bene_per_clinician across T1017 (179.5 vs 275.0), H0031 (76.0 vs 115.42), H0032 (33.0 vs 75.0). Suggest possible root causes.
section_4_cross_cutting.rate_outlier_WRITE: WRITE: 2 sentences noting H2019 is 2x peers while T1015 and T1017 are at market — the premium is code-specific, not organization-wide.
section_4_cross_cutting.revenue_concentration_WRITE: WRITE: 2 sentences noting top 3 service lines (outpatient 40.8%, ACT 26.1%, crisis 23.1%) = 90% of visible revenue, and 49.2% (ACT+crisis) has limited benchmarking.

section_5_caveats.per_claim_vs_per_unit_FIXED: All rate-per-claim figures represent the average payment per claim submission. For time-based codes, a single claim may encompass multiple 15-minute units.
section_5_caveats.modifier_rollup_FIXED: Assessment codes (H0031, H0032) combine all modifier variations into a single per-code figure.
section_5_caveats.residential_gap_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence noting residential per-diem codes are absent.
section_5_caveats.volume_limitations_WRITE: WRITE: 1 sentence noting which codes have fewer than 100 claims.
